meta.note: Single source of truth for the INLAND opportunity pipeline. Written by the Scout (Claude), edited by collaborators via the dashboard, and read by the Producer (Claude). Do not rename fields without updating the dashboard and the _inland playbooks.

opportunities.title: Goethe-Institut — Africa–Europe Partnerships for Culture (Visual Arts / Mobility)
opportunities.budget: Mobility up to €4,000 (travel); Visual Arts strand larger (production)
opportunities.eligibility: Artists/cultural professionals collaborating between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe.
opportunities.fitRationale: Direct territory match for the in-progress Rwanda edition; funds Africa–Europe co-creation. Mobility = scouting trip; Visual Arts = production.
opportunities.notes: Quarterly mobility deadlines (next 15 Sep 2026); Visual Arts strand within €8M 2025–28 programme.

opportunities.title: Goethe-Institut — International Coproduction Fund (IKF), Round 2
opportunities.eligibility: Cross-border collaboration with a German/Goethe partner; INLAND qualifies as EU producer with a co-production partner.
opportunities.fitRationale: Producer review: the IKF funds PERFORMING ARTS ONLY (music, theatre, dance, performance) and explicitly excludes film/visual-arts/publishing — so a standard INLAND magazine edition does not qualify. Only viable as a reframed performing-arts co-production (e.g. Bhand Pather / Dard-Shina music) with a Germany-based theatre partner. Recommend redirecting to the Goethe Africa–Europe Visual Arts fund. See research brief, Producer review.
opportunities.territory: Open — pairs with a new edition co-produced with a local partner

changes.note: Producer started: IKF R2 × Kashmir edition

Stopped: IKF is performing-arts-only per official Goethe guidelines; magazine edition ineligible. Reverted; recommend Africa–Europe fund or a reframed performing-arts co-production. No assets produced.
